evals.name: explicit-slash-command-triggers
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers. Step 0 prints the 8-step plan, Step 1 grep-checks for setup_completed: in CLAUDE.md. If fresh fork (no setup_completed line), proceeds through 8 steps. If already-configured, refuses gracefully and points at /update-config (T2 invariant).

assertions.text: /bootstrap skill triggers
Step 0 narration (welcome + 8-step plan) is emitted as user-visible text before any probes run
Step 1 fork-state check runs first (grep for setup_completed: in CLAUDE.md)
On already-configured fork, skill refuses gracefully and surfaces /update-config alternative (T2)

evals.name: fresh-install-phrase-triggers
evals.prompt: I just cloned this repo, set up Beru for me
evals.expected_output: Natural-language fresh-fork phrasing. Skill triggers via broad first-run intent detection.

assertions.text: /bootstrap skill triggers (NOT /new-project, NOT /update-config)
Step 1 fork-state check runs (grep for setup_completed: in CLAUDE.md)

evals.name: customize-persona-triggers
evals.prompt: I want to customize the persona — I'm not Sid, my name is Alex
evals.expected_output: User signaling fresh fork + persona customization intent. Skill triggers, Step 4 routes to (b) customize, Step 6a regenerates persona files from templates.

assertions.text: /bootstrap skill triggers
Step 4 offers (a) keep default / (b) customize / (c) keep as-is via AskUserQuestion

evals.name: user-edited-soul-md-preserved-not-overwritten
evals.expected_output: T1 INVARIANT TEST. SOUL.md has been edited by the user beyond the original Beru content (e.g., they added a custom Operating Principle). When Step 6a detects divergence via `git show HEAD:SOUL.md` + `diff -q`, it MUST ask before overwriting and default to skip.
preconditions: SOUL.md has been edited beyond the original — e.g., a 7th Operating Principle added that's neither in the upstream Beru SOUL.md nor the template

assertions.text: Step 6a detects SOUL.md divergence via git diff against HEAD
Skill asks user via AskUserQuestion before overwriting — options include skip / show-diff / overwrite / save to .bootstrap-suggested
Default behavior is to skip overwriting and preserve user edits

evals.name: already-configured-fork-refuses-without-reconfigure
evals.expected_output: T2 INVARIANT TEST. CLAUDE.md Configuration section has real values (user.name = 'Alex', user.github = 'alex-rivera') and a setup_completed: 2025-XX-XX flag. Skill must refuse and point at /update-config.
preconditions: CLAUDE.md Configuration section has user.name set to a real value (not placeholder, not 'Sid' default)

assertions.text: Skill detects already-configured state in Step 1 (grep for setup_completed: line)
Skill refuses gracefully — does NOT proceed to Step 2+
Skill surfaces /update-config as the alternative for partial edits
Skill mentions --reconfigure flag for users who genuinely want to redo setup

evals.name: skill-does-not-auto-commit
evals.expected_output: T3 INVARIANT TEST. After all writes complete in Steps 5, 6, 7a the skill surfaces a diff and tells the user to commit manually. NEVER runs `git add` or `git commit`.

assertions.text: Skill never invokes `git add` or `git commit` (T3)
Final message in Step 8 explicitly says 'does not auto-commit'

evals.name: skill-does-not-install-tools
evals.expected_output: T4 INVARIANT TEST. Step 2 (environment detection) is READ-ONLY probes. If gws/gh/MCPs are missing or not authed, skill surfaces hints in the detection panel and Step 8 follow-ups but does NOT install or auth anything.

assertions.text: Step 2 only runs read-only Bash probes (gws auth status / gh auth status / jq .mcp.json)
Step 2 renders a detection panel with ✅/⏳/⚠️/❌ flags for each CLI, MCP, pipeline tool, and persona file
Skill does NOT invoke `brew install`, `gws auth login`, `gh auth login`, or any MCP OAuth flow
For ⚠️ tools, skill surfaces a one-line action hint (e.g., 'Run gws auth login') instead of trying to fix

evals.name: smoke-test-failure-preserves-debugging-artifact
evals.expected_output: Step 7b smoke test invokes /new-project with a throwaway. If the new project's CLAUDE.md still contains <user.name> placeholder (verification failed), DO NOT delete the smoke-test artifact — leave it for debugging.
preconditions: Configuration section may have written incompletely (Step 7a partial — /new-project might still produce a project with <user.name> placeholder)

assertions.text: On smoke-test verification PASS, skill deletes the throwaway project
On smoke-test verification FAIL, skill preserves the artifact and surfaces the path for debugging

evals.name: settings-edit-does-not-trigger-bootstrap
evals.prompt: I want to add a new permission to settings.json
evals.expected_output: Negative test. This is settings.json / hooks territory — that's /update-config. /bootstrap should NOT trigger.

assertions.text: /bootstrap skill does NOT trigger for this prompt
/update-config or settings-related skill should be the right routing

evals.name: new-project-does-not-trigger-bootstrap
evals.prompt: scaffold a new project for the Q2 planning work
evals.expected_output: Negative test. This is /new-project (project scaffolding), not /bootstrap (workspace+persona setup). Different concerns.

assertions.text: /bootstrap skill does NOT trigger for this prompt
/new-project is the correct routing (different scope)
